items.title: 'Where heritage meets heartbeat': Wilderness railway facing uncertain future

children.content: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

gallery.altText: Locomotive travels through a forest railway track.
A heritage stream train beside the platform at a small station amongst bush.
Historical image of a locomotive travelling across wooden bridge.

thumbnail.altText: Locomotive travels through a forest railway track.
A heritage stream train beside the platform at a small station amongst bush.
Historical image of a locomotive travelling across wooden bridge.

items.title: Tas Labor has 'a bit of a shock coming', party elders warn

children.content: Ahead of a state conference, a former Tasmanian Labor premier and treasurer lash the party's performance, telling the ABC the opposition's policies are indistinguishable from the Liberals — and there's no pathway to government without crossbench support.

image.altText: Josh Willie highlighted in red in front of parliament house while David Bartlett and Michael Aird are behind him.

items.title: With most of it not fit to sell, this op shop fixes donated items to reduce landfill

children.content: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

image.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

items.title: Queen Mary of Denmark's father dies in Tasmania aged 84

children.content: The Australian-born Queen of Denmark last visited her father Professor John Donaldson during a royal visit to Tasmania last month.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of an older bearded man facing away from the camera looking pensive.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of an older bearded man facing away from the camera looking pensive.

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on childcare. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise childcare, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

image.altText: A child walks at childcare.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Musicians to wedding businesses, Australians grapple as fuel prices rise

children.content: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran, which has seen fuel prices spike, has created a similar struggles.

image.altText: A woman sitting with a guitar looking away from the camera.

items.title: 'Like a flea on an elephant's back': The unexpected rise of solar power

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

items.title: Bravery awards for heroes 'who stood strong' during Bondi mass stabbing

children.content: Two years on from the Westfield Bondi Junction stabbing attack, eight heroes have been recognised in a special Australian Bravery Decorations honours list.

image.altText: Muhammad Taha sitting on a grey couch wearing a black hoodie.

items.title: Scientists warn of astronomy brain drain after telescope deal is axed

children.content: The Artemis II mission used space technology developed in Australia, but experts fear the axing of a multimillion-dollar telescope partnership may stifle innovation and astronomy.

image.altText: Five buildings, four of which are firing lasers from their rooftops, against a starry Milky Way backdrop.

items.title: The 'extraordinary' rise of Kim Jong Un's mysterious teenage daughter

children.content: North Korea leader Kim Jong Un's 13-year-old daughter is tipped to be next in line. So who is Kim Ju Ae?

gallery.altText: Kim Jue Ae and Kim Jong Un stand side by side in matching leather jackets, looking at something in the distance.

thumbnail.altText: Kim Jue Ae and Kim Jong Un stand side by side in matching leather jackets, looking at something in the distance.

items.title: The recycling project turning used tennis balls into shoes

children.content: What should be done with all those used tennis balls that get inevitably sent to landfill? Lilian Xu might have found the answer.

image.altText: Lilian Xu sits with red shoes next to tennis balls in the stands of Kooyng Tennis Club.

items.title: McIlroy, Day stumble as players' champion takes Masters lead

children.content: A bogey for Australia's Jason Day and an early double from Rory McIlroy, hunting a second straight Masters victory, leaves Cameron Young alone at the top in the final round at Augusta. Follow live.

image.altText: Jason Day grimaces at the Masters

items.title: Quiz: Jump in, we're going back in time

children.content: Which state had Australia's only communist MP? And where did the Titanic sink?

gallery.altText: The RMS Titanic leaves Belfast for sea trials.

thumbnail.altText: The RMS Titanic leaves Belfast for sea trials.

items.title: Love story: 'It's not my kidney now, it's yours. I'm just holding it for you'

children.content: Despite a 10-year age gap and geographical separation, Sajarn and Codey Stow have retained a close bond since childhood. That bond has only strengthened since Codey received a donor kidney from his brother last November.

image.altText: Two smiling First Nations men wearing loud, collared short-sleeve shirts in a tropical setting.

items.title: Is sitting with your legs crossed actually bad for you?

children.content: The healthiest sitting position is often the one you do not hold for the next hour.

image.altText: A woman with faded ripped jeans sitting on a chair with crossed legs

items.title: Trump is the latest to enter a decades-long fight for this nation's gold

children.content: The stockpile is part of a longer skirmish for access to and control of a bonanza playing out an ocean away, in a country clambering to reinvent itself amid political turmoil.

image.altText: Nicolas Maduro in a black suit sitting at a table with his right hand on a pile of six stacked gold bars.

items.title: No work, no savings, no way to wait: India's migrant workers head home

children.content: The war in the Middle East has brought one of India's largest industrial hubs to a standstill. In the city of Morbi, hundreds of thousands of workers have lost their jobs, after gas supplies were disrupted thousands of kilometres away.

image.altText: A close up of an Indian man.

items.title: This viral craze is leaving a trail of battered bodies. Exponents want sporting legitimacy

children.content: Vali stands atop a cliff. He screams, psyching himself up. Three, two, one jump — then in mere seconds he smacks the water 42 metres below, sustaining massive injuries.

gallery.altText: A shirtless man falling sideways through the air.
A large waterfall in Costa Rica.
A young man in a hospuital bed giving two thumbs up.

A young man in a hospuital bed giving two thumbs up.

items.title: Legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies aged 92

children.content: The singer recorded over 12,000 songs and was one of Indian cinema's most celebrated voices.

image.altText: An older woman in traditional indian dress poses on a red carpet for the camera

items.title: Police charge gang rapist who escaped from Sydney hospital five days ago

children.content: On Wednesday, Michael Angok fled on foot from Bankstown Hospital shortly after 9:30am. He had been taken there from Villawood Detention Centre.

image.altText: A composite image of a man's face and the same man running across a road.

items.title: Emergency warning issued after blaze at Perth battery recycling facility

children.content: A man has been taken to hospital and a HAZMAT emergency warning has been issued as a serious fire burns at a lithium-ion battery recycling facility in Perth.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Hiker found 'safe and well' after spending 24 hours lost in Victoria's north-west

children.content: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

image.altText: Police finds missing camper Andy

items.title: The animation on this show was generated by AI. Would you watch it?

children.content: A television show that uses generative artificial intelligence to animate Indonesian folktales is creating a stir.

items.title: Woman, 25, drowns at popular swimming hole near Alice Springs

children.content: The woman died despite resuscitation efforts from paramedics, Northern Territory Police say.

image.altText: Ellery Creek Big Hole is seen on a sunny afternoon.

items.title: New Zealand braces for worst impacts as Cyclone Vaianu makes landfall

children.content: Floods and power outages are reported in New Zealand, as authorities warn the worst damage from Cyclone Vaianu is yet to come.

image.altText: Water across roads and paddocks.

items.title: 'This was not easy': Artemis II crew's first comments

children.content: Artemis crew members have described their mission as "the greatest dream on Earth" after landing on Saturday and setting a new deep-space travel record.

items.title: Why diesel price continues to soar in spite of fuel excise cut

children.content: As the war in the Middle East rages on, diesel prices are continuing to rise despite efforts to stabilise costs. So why is this happening, can anything be done about it and is the worst yet to come?

image.altText: a petrol pump

items.title: Minister casts doubt on road user tax due to EV uptake concerns

children.content: A new tax on road users, including electric vehicles, has been cast into doubt by the transport minister, who says now may not be the time for it.

image.altText: mixed vehicles

items.title: I thought I understood China. Then I ordered a craft beer

children.content: On a midsummer night last year, I took a sip of lager at a craft beer pub in my hometown in China's south. But this was not just a beer. It was a cultural symbol that shows just how much China's Gen Z is changing.

gallery.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

thumbnail.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols
